body.$value: What is the underlying mechanism that lets a director apply different recovery, 2SV, and self-recovery policies to students versus staff?
Placing students and staff in separate organizational units (OUs)
Assigning every student and staff member a different license type
Giving each population a different primary domain
Tagging users with custom directory attributes
For under-13 (COPPA) student accounts, what is the recommended approach to account recovery?
Collect each student's personal phone and email so they can self-recover
Use admin-driven password resets rather than storing personal student recovery phone/email; recovery info is OFF by default for K-12 students
Require students to set up Advanced Protection so recovery is automatic
Share one recovery email across the whole grade level
While investigating a possibly compromised admin account, where in the Admin console do you review configuration changes such as a newly granted admin privilege or changed recovery settings?
Billing > Subscriptions
Directory > Users > Bulk update
Reporting > Audit and investigation > Admin log events
Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail settings
Why does the Alert center rule "User granted Admin privilege" matter for incident response?
It tracks license usage so you can reclaim unused seats
It can signal an attacker self-escalating by granting themselves admin rights
It notifies users that their password is about to expire
It confirms that scheduled backups completed successfully
When you discover a compromised user account, what is the correct FIRST step?
Reset the password first, then decide whether further action is needed
Delete the account immediately to stop the attacker
Suspend the account, which resets sign-in cookies and OAuth tokens
Email the user and wait for them to confirm the activity
Why is a password reset alone insufficient to remediate a compromised account?
Because the new password could be guessed again within minutes
Because existing sessions/tokens, OAuth grants, app passwords, and mail forwarding/filters persist, so you must also revoke tokens and review forwarding and delegation
Because a password reset also deletes the user's mailbox by default
Because password resets are not logged in the audit tools
